violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Bag of meat loaf mix was stored with raw beef in a container in walk-in cooler #2. Corrected on site.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloths for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Cardboard box holding single-use items in the back prep area is not a cleanable surface. Cardboard boxes should be disposed of once they are no longer in use.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The inside of the microwave on the north end of the cook-line needs to be cleaned. The inside of the prep coolers on the cook-line need to be cleaned. The racking in walk-in cooler #1 need to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The fans and fan covers in walk-in cooler #1 need to be cleaned. Evaporator cover in walk-in #1 has rust and mold on it. The tabletops underneath the flat tops need to be cleaned. The fan being stored under the hand sink in the kitchen needs to be cleaned. Walk-in freezer doors doesn't shut tight, bad seals. Ice forming on the edge and outside of the door.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Single-use to-go containers on the microwaves on the cook-line need to be inverted so that the food contact surface is not exposed.--Single-service item was reused or used more than one time. Aluminum pie tins were being washed and re-used, they are a single-use item and should be disposed of after use.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The hand sink in the kitchen was not working properly and the 3 comp sink was leaking at the time of inspection.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not kept clean or maintained. Handwash sink in the kitchen needs to be cleaned thoroughly.
Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. The employee bathroom did not have any soap readily available.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room was not clean or kept in good repair. Toilet in the employee bathroom did not have a cover to the tank.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors and walls throughout the facilty need to be cleaned.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee clothing and items were stored above clean dishes on the server line.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mops were being stored on the floor next to the mop sink and placed in a proper position to dry.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Prep table on the north end of the cook-line had potentially hazardous foods temping between 50-55 degrees. Unit did not have a lid and was covered in plastic wrap.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The gaskets of the reach in coolers on the cook line have a build up and need to be cleaned. The insides of the reach-in cooler need to be cleaned. Microwaves on the line need to be cleaned. Tables on the server line holding coffee containers need to be cleaned.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. To-go containers located on the microwaves need to be inverted.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. No paper towels in the dispenser at the hand sink located in the back.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The floors in the walk-in coolers and freezers need deep cleaning. Floors on the kitchen line and the server line need to be deep cleaned.

Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items. Lighting in the dry storage area did not have the proper shielding on the lights.
Installed, maintained (1 pt deducted)
